name: Clower, Aida
motto: "Grace is in all her steps, In every gesture, dignity."
text: "Grace is in all her steps, In every gesture, dignity."

name: Peters, Mary Lillian
motto: "Graceful and useful all she does, Progressive and blessed where e'er she goes."
text: "Graceful and useful all she does, Progressive and blessed where e'er she goes."

name: Wood, W. H.
motto: "The secret of success, Is constant work."
text: "The secret of success, Is constant work."

name: Trugen, Beard
motto: "She's sweeter than the morning's dawn When rising Phallius is seen, She's spotless like the flowering thorn With flowers so white and leaves so green, It's not her air, her form, her face, Tho' matching Beauty's fabled Queen, 'Tis the mind that shines in every grace And chiefly in her roguish een."
text: "She's sweeter than the morning's dawn When rising Phallius is seen, She's spotless like the flowering thorn With flowers so white and leaves so green, It's not her air, her form, her face, Tho' matching Beauty's fabled Queen, 'Tis the mind that shines in every grace And chiefly in her roguish een."

name: Eckhoff, J. A.
motto: "It is not good for man to live alone, Therefore I'm married."
text: "It is not good for man to live alone, Therefore I'm married."

name: Merkel, Eunice H.
motto: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort, and command."
text: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort, and command."

name: Johnson, William Harvey
motto: "A proper man, as one shall see in a summer's day."
text: "A proper man, as one shall see in a summer's day."

name: Routh, Hollingsworth
motto: "My tongue within my lips I reign, For who talks much must talk in vain."
text: "My tongue within my lips I reign, For who talks much must talk in vain."

name: Combs, Eva
motto: "She must not be so far from what God intended a woman to be."
text: "She must not be so far from what God intended a woman to be."

name: McCleskey, J. W.
motto: "Love is a dream to which marriage is an alarm clock."
text: "Love is a dream to which marriage is an alarm clock."

name: Murff, Thelma Elizabeth
motto: "Girl of the bright brown eyes, Always witty, sweet and wise, Truthful, steadfast, winsome, gay, Thelma goes her laughing way."
text: "Girl of the bright brown eyes, Always witty, sweet and wise, Truthful, steadfast, winsome, gay, Thelma goes her laughing way."

name: Edwards, Albert G.
motto: "Man never is but always to be blest."
text: "Man never is but always to be blest."

name: Wyatt, Leroy Washington
motto: "He achieves success who lives well, laughs often and loves much; who gains the respect of intelligent men and love of little children; who never lacks appreciation of earth's beauty or fails to express it; who always looks for the best in others and gives the best he has."
text: "He achieves success who lives well, laughs often and loves much; who gains the respect of intelligent men and love of little children; who never lacks appreciation of earth's beauty or fails to express it; who always looks for the best in others and gives the best he has."

name: Gallaspy, Allene
motto: "A smooth and steadfast mind, Gentle thoughts and calm desires, Heart with equal love combined Kindle never dying fires. Where these are not, we despise Lovely cheeks or lips or eyes."
text: "A smooth and steadfast mind, Gentle thoughts and calm desires, Heart with equal love combined Kindle never dying fires. Where these are not, we despise Lovely cheeks or lips or eyes."

name: Lott, Ruth
motto: "To hear that girl laugh you'd think she was all fun, But the angels all smile at the good she has done."
text: "To hear that girl laugh you'd think she was all fun, But the angels all smile at the good she has done."

name: Lipscomb, Ruth
motto: "She's a winsome wee thing, She's a handsome wee thing, She's a bonnie wee thing, This sweet wee Ruth of ours."
text: "She's a winsome wee thing, She's a handsome wee thing, She's a bonnie wee thing, This sweet wee Ruth of ours."

name: Thomas, Emmett V.
motto: "Woman is man's bread of life, therefore I'm always hungry."
text: "Woman is man's bread of life, therefore I'm always hungry."

name: Stapleton, Myrtle
motto:
"Gentle in manner, strong in performance,
One who is loved for her smile,
Her grace and look,
That's Myrtle."
clubs: Member Y. W. C. A. Cabinet
text:
"Gentle in manner, strong in performance,
One who is loved for her smile,
Her grace and look,
That's Myrtle."

name: Temple, Mary Dee
motto:
"Strong of mind, massive of heart,
She has always acted well her part."
text:
"Strong of mind, massive of heart,
She has always acted well her part."

name: Allen, Grace
motto: "I love her for her smile, her look, and her way of gentle speaking."
text: "I love her for her smile, her look, and her way of gentle speaking."

name: Clara, Conn
motto: "Happy, thoughtful, kind and true, There is no favor she will not do."
clubs: Y. W. Pres. U. R. in Y. W. C. A.
text: "Happy, thoughtful, kind and true, There is no favor she will not do."

name: Ruth, Millins
motto: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort and command."
text: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort and command."

name: Thelma Elizabeth, Murff
motto: "Girl of the bright brown eyes Always witty, sweet and wise. Truthful, steadfast, winsome, gay, Thelma goes her laughing way."
text: "Girl of the bright brown eyes Always witty, sweet and wise. Truthful, steadfast, winsome, gay, Thelma goes her laughing way."

name: M. Luke, Riley
motto: "The Friendly Politician."
clubs: Chaplain, Sergeant-at-Arms, Vice-President, twice President Presbyterian Literary Society
member of Y. M. C. A. Cabinet and Delegate to Blue Ridge, '21, '22
description: Few students have shown greater promise or left their Alma Mater with a more solid record of achievement than has M. Luke Riley. As a member of the state legislature, he served the Normal College well, being at all times the champion of her rights. He is the author of the bill giving the Normal College the right to grant degrees. Of generous disposition and sterling character, he has made many friends among the students, as is evidenced by the fact that he was elected to the following offices:
text:
"The Friendly Politician."
Few students have shown greater promise or left their Alma Mater with a more solid record of achievement than has M. Luke Riley. As a member of the state legislature, he served the Normal College well, being at all times the champion of her rights. He is the author of the bill giving the Normal College the right to grant degrees. Of generous disposition and sterling character, he has made many friends among the students, as is evidenced by the fact that he was elected to the following offices:

name: Williams, Lucile
motto: "So well to know, Her own that what she wills to do or say Seems wisest, virtuousest, discreetest, best."
text: "So well to know, Her own that what she wills to do or say Seems wisest, virtuousest, discreetest, best."

name: Ramsey, H. G.
motto: "The man who never fails to speak."
description: Creeds die, but deeds live and weave themselves into the souls of men and will ever guide to the right, truth, justice, love and mercy. His personality is most striking. Tall of stature, large of build and big of heart. He commands the respect and love of all. To prove this he has been honored with the following offices:
text:
"The man who never fails to speak."
Creeds die, but deeds live and weave themselves into the souls of men and will ever guide to the right, truth, justice, love and mercy. His personality is most striking. Tall of stature, large of build and big of heart. He commands the respect and love of all. To prove this he has been honored with the following offices:

name: Prophet, Tommie
motto: "Her air has a meaning, her movements a grace, You turn from the fairest to gaze on her face, When you see her forehead and mouth, You see as distinctly her soul and her truth, Such a blue inner light from her eyelids outbreaks You look on her silence and you fancy she speaks."
text: "Her air has a meaning, her movements a grace, You turn from the fairest to gaze on her face, When you see her forehead and mouth, You see as distinctly her soul and her truth, Such a blue inner light from her eyelids outbreaks You look on her silence and you fancy she speaks."

name: Gafford, L. E.
motto: "If worry were the only cause of death he would live forever."
text: "If worry were the only cause of death he would live forever."

name: Holley, Annie Nora
motto: "She doeth little kindnesses which most leave undone, or despise."
clubs: Y. W. C. A.: Bible Committee Chairman
member Y. W. C. A. Cabinet
text: "She doeth little kindnesses which most leave undone, or despise."

name: Gandy, W. S.
motto: "Faithful and earnest, Kindhearted and true, Ready to do his best In all there is to do."
text: "Faithful and earnest, Kindhearted and true, Ready to do his best In all there is to do."

name: Armstrong, Sophia
motto: "Knowledge, truth and virtue were her theme."
text: "Knowledge, truth and virtue were her theme."

name: Brewer, George W.
motto: "The sweet remembrance of the just Shall flourish when he sleeps in dust."
clubs: President of the Clarke County Teachers' Association
description: "Governor," as he is known by his friends, is one of the few who come here during the sixth and seventh terms.
text:
"The sweet remembrance of the just Shall flourish when he sleeps in dust."
"Governor," as he is known by his friends, is one of the few who come here during the sixth and seventh terms.

name: Phillips, Lilah
motto: "Silent when glad; affectionate tho' shy."
text: "Silent when glad; affectionate tho' shy."

name: Kersh, H. H.
motto: "Oh! Why should life all labor be and girls and things that bother me."
description: He is at his best when driving a car with one hand on a dark night.
text:
"Oh! Why should life all labor be and girls and things that bother me."
He is at his best when driving a car with one hand on a dark night.

name: Tidwell, Colleen
motto: "She is more precious than rubies; and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared to her."
text: "She is more precious than rubies; and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared to her."

name: Minton, A. S.
motto: "His heart and hands are both open and both free, For what he has he gives to serve his friends."
clubs: Member Y. M. C. A. Cabinet
Secretary and Chaplain Prestonian Literary Society
text: "His heart and hands are both open and both free, For what he has he gives to serve his friends."

name: Williams, Clyde
motto: "Those about her, from her shall read the perfect ways of honor."
text: "Those about her, from her shall read the perfect ways of honor."

name: Dunlap, Milus Dorsey
motto: "His justifiable pride—his wife, his knowledge and skill of his profession, and his truly Christian character."
clubs: President of State Student Council Y. M. C. A., '23
text: "His justifiable pride—his wife, his knowledge and skill of his profession, and his truly Christian character."

name: Hardy, Sudie
motto: "A fair girl of eighteen, fresh, glittering with graces, of mind and mien."
text: "A fair girl of eighteen, fresh, glittering with graces, of mind and mien."

name: Craft, H. M.
motto: "Since climbing is good and I enjoy it so why stop short of the top?"
description: Craft is one of the few men that has made a success of teaching in his home community.
text:
"Since climbing is good and I enjoy it so why stop short of the top?"
Craft is one of the few men that has made a success of teaching in his home community.

name: Boykin, Eddie Mae
motto: "You'd have known her by the merriment that sparkled in her eyes."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Publicity Committee
text: "You'd have known her by the merriment that sparkled in her eyes."

name: Fuqua, H. G.
motto: "It isn't so much what a man stands for, it's what he falls for."
text: "It isn't so much what a man stands for, it's what he falls for."

name: Walton, Mabel
motto: "Smiling in her work, Singing in her play, And going along happy All of the day."
text: "Smiling in her work, Singing in her play, And going along happy All of the day."

name: Rhodes, M. C.
motto: "Nothing is humbler than ambition when it's about to climb."
text: "Nothing is humbler than ambition when it's about to climb."

name: Brewer, Minnie
motto: "For she is wise, if I can judge her; And fair she is if that mine eyes be true; And true she is as she has proved to herself."
text: "For she is wise, if I can judge her; And fair she is if that mine eyes be true; And true she is as she has proved to herself."

name: Fillingame, Ola
motto: "To know her is to love and trust her."
description: Her record has been among the highest here in college.
text:
"To know her is to love and trust her."
Her record has been among the highest here in college.

name: Long, S. A.
motto: "By deed, not words, his noble traits are ever brought to light, his friendship—that to be desired and prized."
clubs: Vice-President and Critic and Chaplain of Literary Society
text: "By deed, not words, his noble traits are ever brought to light, his friendship—that to be desired and prized."
